tasks.title: Address context-rotting static files
tasks.notes: decisions.md, session-history.md, pickup.md rot between sessions — replace with maintainable alternatives

tasks.title: Evaluate OpenClaw for Tier 2 watcher agents
tasks.notes: Persistent intelligent agents for session-history and decisions updates

tasks.title: Explore .claude directory for vault integration
tasks.notes: Investigate transcript files, memories, settings, and other .claude contents. Determine what can be incorporated into the vault system for better context persistence.

tasks.title: Research MCP servers for vault system
tasks.notes: Look into MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers — could provide structured tool access to vault operations, task tracking, and context loading for any Claude interface.

tasks.title: Research sub-agents for vault automation
tasks.notes: Investigate Claude Code sub-agents and Agent SDK for automating vault operations — potential path for Tier 2 watcher agents, context maintenance, and multi-step workflows.

tasks.title: Track chat sessions per project (transcript linkage)
tasks.notes: Track which Claude sessions interacted with each project. Link to specific transcript .jsonl files. Need to solve auto-detection of current session ID. Design: per-project sessions.json or extend event log.

tasks.title: Add provenance tracking to tasks (transcript + message linkage)
tasks.notes: When tasks are created or modified, record which transcript file and ideally which message triggered the change. This creates a traceable chain from task back to the conversation that spawned it. Could store as metadata on each task entry (created_from, modified_from fields) pointing to transcript:message_id pairs.

tasks.title: Transform ML OS demo into agent-facing context pack builder
tasks.notes: CLI-first, not UI-first. Any agent should be able to compose context packs via commands — select kernel, schema, scenario, attach vault sources, stamp with provenance, output a deployable seed prompt. The web UI is secondary. Priority: (1) CLI compose toolchain (2) API endpoints for remote agents (3) Optional web composer. The created agent's context pack should reference the transcript/session where it was designed. Ties to t-fa0b53 (provenance) and t-b7e972 (session tracking).
